description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a domed top and a cylindrical body, featuring prominent black bolts around the top and bottom flanges. The hydrant has two side hose connection outlets and one larger front outlet, each secured with round caps, some of which are attached to short metal chains. The surface is painted red but shows signs of weathering and wear, with visible scratches and patches of faded paint. A small operating nut is present at the very top, used for opening the hydrant valve. There are subtle manufacturer or model markings embossed on the rear side of the body. The overall construction appears robust, made primarily of cast metal, designed to withstand urban outdoor conditions.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with three hose outlets, black bolts, and metal chains securing the caps. The surface shows signs of use and has embossed markings on the rear.
description_view_0: The hydrant is seen from a front angle, highlighting the large central outlet and the operating nut on top, with a chain hanging from the cap.
description_view_1: This view shows the hydrant head-on, with the large front outlet and both side outlets visible, each with attached chains.
description_view_2: The left side of the hydrant is visible, showing a side outlet with its cap and chain, and the cylindrical body with weathered paint.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the back of the hydrant, including embossed manufacturer or model markings and both side outlets with chains.
